programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.desc: The vengeance of a jealous father and the terror of a deadly epidemic trap Richard Kimble

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former marine falls to his death from a hotel balcony, and investigations quickly lead to the discovery of another body. As Gibbs and the team dig deeper, they uncover evidence that points to the activities of a vice ring trafficking young girls. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A special agent disappears during an undercover assignment, forcing the team to work alongside the LAPD in a case that involves human-trafficking, money-laundering and drugs. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself in an unusual and dangerous situation when three children decide to use him as an ally in their efforts to run away from an orphanage.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man is fatally stabbed outside a coffee shop, and the detectives suspect a local homeless man. However, the conviction is threatened on appeal when it is revealed Cerreta and Logan failed to secure a warrant to search his home - a lean-to in Central Park

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A rookie officer goes undercover as a drug dealer in an attempt to keep heroin out of schools, but his assignment becomes complicated when he meets an old friend

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy captain wearing a colourful costume underneath his uniform is shot and killed in a flower shop, and further investigation reveals he was leading a double life as a masked vigilante. Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The officers race against time to track down a highly specialised hit squad that has murdered an agent on assignment in Cyprus, desperate to intervene before a second target is killed. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. The circumstances surrounding the man's demise lead the investigators to suspect someone was secretly trying to drive him insane, bringing them into contact with Samantha Ryan, the director of a division specialising in psychological warfare. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team tries to find the culprit behind an ambush on a vehicle transporting marines in the desert, which resulted in the death of one man and abduction of two others. Meanwhile, Nate returns from his mystery assignment

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: The girls visit an antiques market held in an old warehouse, but somehow get locked in. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and the resistance attempt to smuggle the airmen back to England inside empty landmines, but Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen hide in the mines awaiting resistance members

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy dons a Robin Hood outfit, and promptly sets Nora's heart a-flutter - much to the frustration of Compo, who responds by getting a costume of his own. Rural comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton, with Keith Clifford and Kathy Staff

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Horticultural lecturer Rosemary Boxer is hired by a wealthy but ill businessman to help cure a disease causing his trees to lose their bark. She gets to know Laura Thyme, who turns up after a friend is killed in a suspicious car accident, and when the pair find the lord of the manor unconscious beneath his bedroom window, they must decide whether depression led him to attempt suicide or if there is a more sinister explanation. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal, Pam Ferris, David Mallinson and Roger Alborough

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The amateur sleuths uncover the remains of a racehorse while engaged on horticultural work in the grounds of pop star Nev Connolly's supposedly haunted mansion. Before long, gruesome revelations are coming thick and fast as Nev is shot dead and more bones are discovered - this time belonging to a young woman. As they investigate further, Rosemary and Laura are plunged into a sinister mystery with roots deep in the past. Drama, guest starring Murray Head (Heartbeat) and Abigail Cruttenden (Benidorm)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: When a retrospective exhibition of murdered fashion designer Ritchie Levene's work opens, the team is prompted to re-examine the three-year-old crime. The detectives learn about his tempestuous relationships with his new spouse, personal assistant and ex-wife Sarah, who believes his brother Adrian is the killer because he had the most to gain from his death. Meanwhile, Gerry enlists Emily to help update his image, although not everyone is impressed with the results. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Hannah Waterman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Quinn and his MI5 team search for a consignment of bombs lost between Ireland and the UK - and the subsequent death of a family planning expert makes them suspect dangerously unbalanced pro-life terrorist Mary Kane. Further investigations reveal she may have plans to detonate as many as 20 of the explosives as part of her campaign. Drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen, Keeley Hawes and Peter Firth

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Four bodies are found in the Thames after a boat full of illegal immigrants founders. Realising they are dealing with a people-trafficking operation gone disastrously wrong, Leo, Harry and Nikki set out to discover whether there are any survivors, while delving into the victims' pasts. Feature-length episode, guest starring Burt Kwouk

Comedy, with Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the drama. She agrees to investigate but, as her inquiries get under way, another body is discovered. Joan Hickson, Gwen Watford and Moray Watson star

programme.desc: The team reopens the case of criminal Mark Johnson, who was killed in a fire at London's Union club in 1996. Drama, guest starring Roy Marsden, Martin Marquez and Tom Watt

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team takes on three cases at once in-between major murder investigations. Starring Tom Ward, William Gaminara and Emilia Fox

programme.desc: Ricky's trying to patch things up with Bianca, whilst she and Tiffany plan more nights out at his expense. Binnie isn't happy at the salon, and Nellie gives more money to David

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: Barrister James Kavanagh (John Thaw) tries to clear an Oxbridge undergraduate accused of raping a housewife, but his home life begins to interfere with work and the cracks in his marriage to independent and successful wife Lizzie re-emerge. First episode of the legal drama, guest starring Ewan McGregor, with Alison Steadman, Lisa Harrow and Anna Chancellor

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The former leader of an Irish Republican group comes forward with information regarding the abduction and subsequent disappearance of an 18-year-old woman in 1983, prompting the team to reopen the case. The reformed dissident claims the rebels used the kidnapping to raise their profile, but his subsequent reinvention as a politician and man of peace leaves Sandra and her colleagues suspicious of his motives. They also investigate whether the arms-manufacturing activities of the victim's father could have made her a target. Crime drama, guest starring Sian Phillips and Annette Crosbie, with Amanda Redman and James Bolam

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: While undercover on a lunch date and routine check at the State Consulate of Turkey, Zoe finds herself in the middle of a siege led by the Kurdish Liberation Army. However, the attack turns out to be a ruse masking a much bigger operation led by a former MI5 agent. Drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry's burgeoning relationship with Nikki is jeopardised when his former girlfriend Penny is killed in a car crash. It looks like suicide at first glance, but DCI Ambrose uncovers evidence that the victim may have had an affair - as well as a secret child. Meanwhile, Leo examines the staged death of a conceptual artist

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Heno Aur
programme.desc: Angharad Mair and Siân Thomas host a programme celebrating 30 years of Heno

programme.title: Be' Ti'n Gwylio?
programme.desc: Gwen and Ben, a mother and son from Ammanford, the Cambourne family from Nelson and a team of five Teulu'r Garth compete against each other from the comfort of their own homes for the chance to win a takeaway of their choice

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd
programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Bwrdd i Dri
programme.desc: Cooking show featuring celebrity contestants as they prepare a three-course meal. Taking part in this edition are Daniel Lloyd, Dylan Jones and Lois Cernyw

programme.title: Ralïo+
programme.desc: The Rally Estonia. Action from the fourth round of the World Rally Championship campaign, as the season resumed after a break of nearly six months

programme.title: Seiclo: Le Tour de France
programme.desc: Action from stage 11 of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 167.5km route from Chatelaillon-Plage to Poitiers

programme.title: Goreuon Gwesty Aduniad
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable reunions of Gwesty Aduniad, and a chat with some of the people featured as it is revealed what happened after their reunion

programme.title: Trysorau Cymru: Tir, Tai a Chyfrinachau
programme.desc: Part one of two. The stories hidden within the walls of ancient houses and an opportunity to question their past - the good and the bad. There are stories of eccentric, revealing characters, who built grand houses as symbols of their grandeur and status. Despite their uncomfortable past, these buildings are part of the nation's history and tell an important story about our culture, society and architecture. With Elinor Gray Williams and Tudur Owen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits a fake motorway used for training by emergency services in Gloucestershire, while Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police try to recover what they suspect are stolen vehicles. Plus, how seized assets are sold off when a ring and a very prestigious Bentley are put up for auction

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the case of a vulnerable tenant trapped in a collapsing property, and meets a resident who has had enough of the abandoned house next door

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama created by Roland Moore in 2009 to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the outbreak of the Second World War. As Britain struggles to contain the Nazi war machine, four females from different walks of life join the Women's Land Army, little realising their decision could change their futures. In the first episode, Nancy, who was forced to enlist, tries to get closer to Lord Hoxley at a dance. Starring Christine Bottomley, Summer Strallen, Becci Gemmell and Jo Woodcock, with Nathaniel Parker and Mark Benton

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine the history behind some of the nation's most famous dishes, beginning by exploring how the monarchy has influenced British cuisine. The duo also prepare a classic coronation chicken, and a beef dish named after Prince Albert, and food historian Ivan Day whips up a meal using a cookbook from the time of James II

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A luxury hotel chain makes penniless and exhausted couple Mike and Alison an offer they can't refuse, but the prospect of their departure drives a wedge between the ghosts, and the hardline Captain finds himself ostracised, while Pat is voted in as reluctant leader. As the clueless spirits brainstorm money-making schemes, the Captain takes matters into his own hands. Meanwhile, fortune smiles on Headless Humphrey in his ceaseless search for his errant body

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Detective Moses Mountree returns to Midsomer, an African warlord faces a new threat, and crooner Thaddeus Price sings a romantic song to his girlfriend

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The three remaining queens write, record and perform an all-new remix of a RuPaul classic as they battle for the title of Canada's first Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Independent Group MP Heidi Allen and stand-up Phil Wang joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a rise in attacks on homeless people, while Rav Wilding talks to a former firefighter who has turned her life around after ending up on the streets

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a three-bedroom house in Worcester and Dion Dublin sees a tiny basement flat in Liverpool. Plus, a revisit to a London property first featured in 2016

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police officers in the Northeast target suspected drug gangs, and hold an auction of seized items. Plus, how property seized from criminals are used to fund children's activities

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a woman who has lived without hot water for a decade in a rented property on Merseyside riddled with damp and mould

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge as Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Plymouth

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Annie learns Bea is pregnant, and enlists the aid of the farmer's son Billy to track down the father - a GI who refuses to take responsibility for his actions. Meanwhile, Tucker suspects Nancy of being an enemy sympathiser, and a dilemma concerning an injured horse drives a wedge between Lord Hoxley and his wife Ellen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the nation's love of bread, tracing its development over the course of centuries. In addition to trying their hands at bagel-making, they create their own version of summer pudding, and prepare a soup-and-roll combination

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer from London to find a new home in Dumfries and Galloway, and also heads for the hills on a Scottish gold rush

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help the Rev Karen and husband Colin, who have £6,000 to turn their oddly-shaped and weed-filled garden in Romsey into an Italian retreat

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a bus conductor's ticket machine, a Victorian automaton that can play the piano, and a former world champion's kickboxing trophy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and greengrocer Chris Bavin return to help more families with their food shopping, beginning with the Macbeth family in Windsor. Single mum Holly has her hands full looking after her two sons - 16-year-old Spencer and Fletcher, who is 11. Caring for Spencer, who has cerebral palsy, leaves Holly little time to cook, and as a result the family has become dependent on microwavable ready meals. While Spencer is happy to eat most foods, Fletcher - who has recently been diagnosed with autism - is fussier

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Cases include Tia, who suffers from eczema and has tried all sorts of homeopathic remedies with little success, and Casey, who just wants his psoriasis under control

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Psoriasis sufferer Megan feels like she has exhausted all treatment options, while Rowanne is interested in learning about how to camouflage her port wine stain

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: Bolton's funniest stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports live from a specialist police rehab unit in Berkshire, and Rav Wilding finds out about the lengths farmers will go to protect animals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A Merseyside drug dealer has his BMW seized and sold off at auction. Meanwhile, police in Derby are after a man they suspect is part of a 'county lines' drug operation

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Liverpool City Council's housing team investigate a rental property that is so dangerous for its residents it has to be immediately shut down

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: After three months, Annie is still struggling to come to terms with Harry's death, Nancy and Lawrence embark on a full-blown affair and Joyce faces a dilemma about her husband. Esther fears Nancy may be a Nazi collaborator and food rations officer Ernest Luckhurst arrives at Finch's farm intent on catching him out

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history of the Vegetarian Society and discover how the movement changed public attitudes and became part of the mainstream. They also consider how British gardening has influenced the nation's cuisine, and provide a few recipes along the way

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life in the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and a couple of gold plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Life at Colchester Zoo for curator Sarah Forsyth, who heads into the busiest summer of her life keeping her animals happy while four out of five of her keeping staff are furloughed. The documentary follows Sarah for two weeks, entirely inside the walls of one of Britain's largest zoos, as she works tirelessly to deal with the summer baby boom, animal emergencies and unique situations created by the absence of paying public

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels off-base for the high society wedding of Abigail's cousin. Raelle throws herself into her relationship with Scylla, who, unbeknownst to Raelle, faces an impossible choice. Abigail becomes disillusioned with her privilege while amongst the notable Bellweather line. Tally gets hit with two life-altering revelations that change the very fabric of the unit

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail has to come to terms with her vulnerability in the aftermath of a horrific event. Tally struggles with being supportive, while also keeping a secret from her unit. Raelle takes extreme measures to connect with Scylla

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: It is the tiger cubs' first birthday bash, and Ben Fogle and Kate Humble look for signs that Longleat Safari Park's Southern Koala breeding programme - the only one in Europe - has resulted in a joey. Meanwhile, Jean Johansson joins keeper James to take a closer look at the anatomy of Gwen - a huge hairy tarantula

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich try to breathe life into a concrete courtyard in Greater Manchester. Nick and Dinah want cottage plants, vegetable beds and room for family meals

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's tour reaches its final stop, Singapore, where he learns about the island city's maritime origins and the vision of the 19th-century Englishman who put it on the map. At the bar of the Raffles Hotel, he treats himself to a Singapore Sling, now a world-famous cocktail, takes a boat down the river and dines with an artist and his family in Chinatown

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of television interviews with actor David Niven, examining the appearances on chat shows he made over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Death (1946)
programme.desc: A Second World War bomber pilot survives jumping out of his stricken plane without a parachute and falls in love with the radio operator he had first spoken to during the fateful flight. A heavenly messenger is sent to tell him he should have died and that his presence is required in the afterlife, but he remains determined to continue his life on Earth and insists on a trial to resolve the issue. Powell and Pressburger's romantic fantasy, starring David Niven, Kim Hunter, Roger Livesey and Marius Goring

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Shane Richie takes an aerial journey over London to explore the effect of the Blitz on his home city, retracing the routes used by the Luftwaffe and talking to survivors

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Ricky Tomlinson takes an aerial journey over Liverpool to explore the effect the Blitz had on his home city from the perspective of those that dropped the bombs. He also hears from survivors on the ground, including his own brother, about how they coped in the city's greatest hour of need

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrel and Catherine Southon find intricately carved walnuts and a silver epergne at Bletchley Park, Buckinghamshire, while Paul Martin meets Second World War codebreakers

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farmers and traditional horsemen Paul from Bedfordshire and John from Hampshire show the skill and passion needed to do succeed in one of the country's top ploughing competitions

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Poet Pam Ayres and her friend and actor Geoffrey Whitehead join experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss on a quest to find items to sell at an auction in Cirencester, Gloucestershire. Their search takes them through Hampshire and Berkshire, with Pam learning about the heroic exploits of the Glider Pilot Regiment and Geoffrey stopping in Southampton for a game on the world's oldest bowling green

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: This time Tom Kerridge tackles special treats, offering ideas for dishes that will hit the spot with some of the serious food-lovers and restaurant-goers among his group of dieters. The calorie-counted recipes he has created include a one-pot Mediterranean pasta dish, a rich and creamy beef stroganoff, and coffee and chocolate custard pots

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The eighth first-round match sees four students from the University of York pit their wits against a team from King's College London in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to treat two more patients living with debilitating symptoms that have baffled other doctors for years. The first patient is 64-year-old army veteran Neil, whose body is covered in a painful rash that itches so intensely he has scratched the top layer of his skin off. The second case is 35-year-old nursing assistant Katie, who has suffered a dramatic decline in health in the last 18 months and has lost four-and-a-half stone

programme.title: Inside Chelsea: Britain's Wealthiest Borough
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig investigates the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea, an area that boasts the UK's most expensive houses and where annual salaries are three times the national average. Her guide is a true Chelsea insider - Amanda Eliasch, who made much of her fortune in a divorce from her billionaire husband 14 years ago. She's also a successful photographer, and achieved notoriety in 2019 when she hired an auction house to dispose of the items in her wardrobe - including 250 pairs of designer shoes

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedy performers Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson delve into the archive to parody a 1970s film on what to do at a business meeting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Artists create pieces for buyers in Chesterfield who want something special to remember their beloved fathers and a stepdad, who they lost within the space of two years

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don offers tips on maintaining the vegetable plot for continuous crops. From her garden in Devon, Carol Klein gives a comprehensive guide to getting plants for free, turning her attention to climbers like honeysuckle and clematis. In Brighton, cameras return to Kate Bradbury's garden to see how the once barren plot is being used by wildlife. Plus, from the archives, Rachel de Thame's visit to Gloucestershire's Kiftsgate Court Gardens, where she heard about the three generations of female gardeners who have made their mark there

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Huddersfield Giants, Warrington Wolves v Hull FC and Salford Red Devils v Castleford Tigers

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Triathlon
programme.desc: Action from the elite races, as the titles were decided in a one-off event following the cancellations of the other stages due to the coronavirus pandemic

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp (1943)
programme.desc: A Home Guard officer serving during the Second World War is outraged by the disrespect for tradition shown by younger soldiers. He looks back on his military adventures during the Boer War and First World War, as well as his three great loves. Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger's satirical drama, starring Roger Livesey, Deborah Kerr, Anton Walbrook and John Laurie

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Ulster Folk and Transport Museum in Belfast, and also visits a family business that builds racing cars. With experts Mark Stacey and Caroline Hawley

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farming families showing ingenuity and determination are up against one another for the Farmers Weekly Diversification of the Year award

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Brian Conley and Nick Owen hunt for antiques in Worcestershire and Shropshire, assisted by experts Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell. A Sinatra figurine, a Georgian toddy ladle and a Morecambe and Wise poster attract much interest, and Kate and Brian learn why brave Shropshire men have played a part in some of the most significant moments in history

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers settle their livestock into winter housing as the first frosts come in. In the Lake District, vet Dani prepares to perform pregnancy tests on all her cows - while six months pregnant herself. The outcome of the tests will determine the future income of the farm. High on the uplands of Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare to undertake a new venture, and in Aberdeenshire, Isla and George fight to save a dangerously dehydrated deer calf

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: A new redevelopment scheme is launched on the edge of the city centre, intended to create nearly 15,000 new properties, but leaving many residents afraid their homes will be demolished to make way for tower blocks. Among those at risk is a woman who is caring for her elderly bed-bound father, who urgently needs to know how long they can stay in the family home. At the West Tower, interior designer Sarah works on a penthouse for a millionaire client, spending thousands of pounds on bespoke furniture

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: An evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London. Jack Whitehall is the man in charge, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Canadian comedienne Katherine Ryan and American jokester Rich Hall

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the final T20 of the three-match series, which came from Ageas Bowl in Southampton, as the sides continued preparations for the ICC T20 World Cup, which gets under way in Australia next month

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to treat two more patients living with debilitating symptoms that have baffled other doctors for years. The first patient is 64-year-old army veteran Neil, whose body is covered in a painful rash that itches so intensely he has scratched the top layer of his skin off. The second case is 35-year-old nursing assistant Katie, who has suffered a dramatic decline in health in the last 18 months and has lost four-and-a-half stone

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness look back at highlights of the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring Freddie trying to bungee-jump a car off a dam, Paddy proving an old hearse can be the ultimate family car, and Chris remembering the iconic Lotus 79 race car

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson and Kirsty Wilson both show how to grow plants for free from seeds and cuttings while Chris Beardshaw demonstrates how to summer prune a mature tree. There is also a film from Calum Cluny, the youngest allotment holder in Leven, whose plot is bursting at the seams with colour and produce

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace recall some of the best - and the worst - reviews of the celebrities' creations. From members of the Women's Institute to previous finalists and the forthright opinions of the restaurant critics, the role of the external opinion is a crucial one. John and Gregg also revisit a classic challenge from 2011, in which the five semi-finalists were tasked with cooking the much-loved recipes from residents of a retirement village

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help the Rev Karen and husband Colin, who have £6,000 to turn their oddly-shaped and weed-filled garden in Romsey into an Italian retreat

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a bus conductor's ticket machine, a Victorian automaton that can play the piano, and a former world champion's kickboxing trophy

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms in Scotland and the Lake District. In Armadale, a hill farmer prepares for one of Europe's biggest sheep sales, while in Haweswater, a couple take on the biggest gamble of their lives by moving to a larger farm 10 miles north. An Aberdeenshire couple who began farming four years ago plan to raise baby ostriches, and a Northumberland shepherd prepares for the National English Sheepdog trials, and new farmers hatch ostriches

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin returns with the antiques-buying challenge in which people are shown the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown. In the first edition, hairdresser Sarah faces off against civil servant Marika in Liverpool

programme.title: The Cruel Sea (1953)
programme.desc: The veteran commander of a British escort vessel guides his inexperienced crew through the dangers of combat at sea during the Second World War Atlantic campaigns. Demoralised by the failure of his last command, the captain faces a life-or-death decision in his duty to sink an enemy vessel. Nautical action adventure, starring Jack Hawkins, Donald Sinden, Stanley Baker and Denholm Elliott

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Myleene Klass takes an aerial journey over Norwich to explore the effect the Blitz had on her home city from the perspective of those that dropped the bombs

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A compilation edition, featuring finds at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court. Plus, Paul Martin uncovers a fascinating family history in Cornwall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Cornish poultry farmer Rebecca Tonks hopes to attract attention to her new egg products at the Speciality Fine Food Fair at London's Olympia. Northampton-based Tom and Tina Warner aim to impress potential buyers with their selection of farm produced gins. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Former cricketer David Gower and comedian and TV presenter Nick Hancock tour Hampshire for antiques and collectibles that will raise them money at auction in Sidcup, with expert help from Charlie Ross and Philip Serrell. Along the way, David and Charlie board HMS Warrior, and Nick and Phil go hunting for brown trout

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the opening episode, Mary heads back to the city where she learnt to cook, Paris, where she visits one of the city's creperies to finesse her technique. Later, inspired by pastries in Parisian patisserie windows, she makes her own brioche and apple frangipane tart

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the first episode, Nadiya kicks things off with a series of her favourite recipes for classic bakes with a twist, including a blueberry scone pizza, and a spicy Asian take on a traditional toad-in-the-hole

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Lydia Quigley is forced to move her girls to the establishment of her old ally, Mrs May. Meanwhile, Charlotte is playing a dangerous game with the Fitzwilliams. Period drama set in London in 1763 following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Blayne enjoys a very expensive night with Charlotte, but is shocked to find out that Isabella has invited both her and Mrs Quigley to his card party. Meanwhile, Margaret is determined to get vengeance on Lydia, and persuades Justice Hunt to take action. Starring Julian Rhind-Tutt and Jessica Brown Findlay

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: A new redevelopment scheme is launched on the edge of the city centre, intended to create nearly 15,000 new properties, but leaving many residents afraid their homes will be demolished to make way for tower blocks. Among those at risk is a woman who is caring for her elderly bed-bound father, who urgently needs to know how long they can stay in the family home. At the West Tower, interior designer Sarah works on a penthouse for a millionaire client, spending thousands of pounds on bespoke furniture

programme.title: The Trial of Alex Salmond
programme.desc: In March this year, Scotland's former First Minister Alex Salmond emerged from the High Court in Edinburgh cleared of 14 charges of sexual misconduct, with the verdict concluding one of the most dramatic trials Britain has seen. This programme sees Kirsty Wark travelling across Scotland as she follows the trial and interviews people who were present at key moments surrounding the political scandal, and looking back at Salmond's political career and the growing rift between him and Nicola Sturgeon

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A stone carver, a fine art painter and a heraldic artist pitch ideas for a piece of art to celebrate the 850th anniversary of a Scottish castle. Hosted by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the murder of a military veteran by his estranged wife in 1825

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the case of a woman executed for killing her husband in 1856, which inspired Thomas Hardy's Tess of the d'Urbervilles

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a mountain against the backdrop of a clear, blue sky

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade concludes his journey by exploring southern Africa. In Mozambique, he discovers that a declining tourist trade has benefited wildlife and explores local attitudes to disability. Moving on to South Africa, he investigates how much life has changed in the 25 years since the downfall of apartheid. His journey ends in Zimbabwe, a country that is still struggling economically despite having vast quantities of gold

programme.title: Africa Turns the Page: The Novels That Shaped a Continent
programme.desc: Nigerian-born presenter and historian David Olusoga explores the story of the African novel, and a time when writers including Chinua Achebe, Ngugi wa Thiong'o and Wole Soyinka became the conscience of a continent. In their wake, the African novel was to spread around the world - writers of the African diaspora such as Buchi Emecheta and Ben Okri created masterpieces from their adopted home of the United Kingdom. Featuring contributions by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, Aminatta Forna and 2019 Booker winner Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The first of three programmes about the wildlife, landscape and culture in different areas of the country explores the Sierra Madre mountains, and reveals how this rugged landscape has shaped the nation. This chain of peaks includes several volcanoes and is home to a wide variety of animals, including black bears, monarch butterflies, orchid bees and quetzals. The programme also visits the ruins of an ancient Aztec shrine and the blue agave plant which grows in the mountains is used in making tequila

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary using archive performance footage and interviews to explore the life of Miriam Makeba, the Johannesburg-born singer and civil rights activist who was forced into exile in 1960 for opposing the apartheid regime. Miriam became one of the first South African musicians to make a name for herself on the international scene, and she used her music to deliver messages against racism and poverty

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Michelangelo was not merely a giant of the Renaissance. He was also one of the era's most controversial personalities. Art historian Sona Datta explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Sona reveals how TV has tried to reconcile Michelangelo's art with his difficult personality, bringing to life the story of a man who rose from humble beginnings to favoured artist of the rich and powerful

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Tyrone native and Irish dance fitness coach Angela Mohan puts her army of athletes through the drill in the lead up to the World Championships. Documentary series in which cameras follow dancing advocates in an attempt to illustrate the vibrant and progressive world they inhabit

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a mountain against the backdrop of a clear, blue sky

programme.title: Africa Turns the Page: The Novels That Shaped a Continent
programme.desc: Nigerian-born presenter and historian David Olusoga explores the story of the African novel, and a time when writers including Chinua Achebe, Ngugi wa Thiong'o and Wole Soyinka became the conscience of a continent. In their wake, the African novel was to spread around the world - writers of the African diaspora such as Buchi Emecheta and Ben Okri created masterpieces from their adopted home of the United Kingdom. Featuring contributions by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, Aminatta Forna and 2019 Booker winner Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a west coast seascape, with turbulent waves, jagged cliffs and dark night skies

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines films from the Horizon archive to discover what science can teach people about dogs. He investigates the origins of domestic breeds and learns about the role humans played in shaping canine evolution, before asking why the bond between mankind and dogs is particularly strong - and whether it can be improved in the future

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats and dogs, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. This first edition focuses on the dog, revealing that the emotional bond between human and canine is so profound that it is helping transform the lives of hardened criminals in the US prison system convicted of the most violent crimes. The programme also hears from Callie Truelove, a girl living with the rare genetic condition Williams syndrome, which makes her extremely loving and sociable. Geneticists have discovered that the same mutations that give Callie her super social nature have also been found in dogs. Narrated by Mark Benton

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: Exploring the forests of the Yucatan Peninsula, which contain the ruins of the Mayan civilisation and a wide range of wildlife, including jaguars, monkeys and tropical birds. The region also holds a secret that is the key to how Mexico's people and animals are able to survive during the country's dry season

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The series concludes by exploring the north of the country, which is dominated by two deserts - the Sonoran and the Chihuahuan. The programme explores the conditions that have shaped this environment, and reveals how animals including prairie dogs, pygmy owls and aplomado falcons have adapted to thrive in these arid conditions

programme.title: Cornwall's Native Poet: Charles Causley
programme.desc: Profile of the poet, who lived his entire life in the Cornish town of Launceston with his mother, and wrote verse that documented the changes in the town through two world wars, as well as his thoughts on local history, mythology, animals and faith

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a west coast seascape, with turbulent waves, jagged cliffs and dark night skies

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Field Marshal Haig plans a new offensive against the Germans, and Capt Blackadder develops a keen interest in art to avoid the bloodshed. However, his newly discovered talents prove so useful to Military Intelligence that he finds himself stuck in the middle of No Man's Land with only his sidekicks for company. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Captain Blackadder is put on trial for killing and eating General Melchett's carrier pigeon. The only thing standing between him and death by firing squad is the finest lawyer in England - but when Baldrick misdirects his request for legal representation, he ends up with George instead. Rowan Atkinson stars in Ben Elton and Richard Curtis's comedy, with Tony Robinson and Stephen Fry

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats and dogs, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. This first edition focuses on the dog, revealing that the emotional bond between human and canine is so profound that it is helping transform the lives of hardened criminals in the US prison system convicted of the most violent crimes. The programme also hears from Callie Truelove, a girl living with the rare genetic condition Williams syndrome, which makes her extremely loving and sociable. programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: A hot summer swimming spot is the latest painting to take shape under the brush of American artist Bob Ross

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts and Matt Williams visit archaeological digs across the country, beginning with a look at the work being carried out in the west of Britain. The presenters visit Salisbury Museum, finding out about developments at sites including the enormous lost Welsh city of Trellech and Kent's Cavern, a prehistoric cave where Britain's earliest human remains have been found

programme.title: Jumbo: The Plane That Changed the World
programme.desc: Documentary examining the origins and development of the Boeing 747, which was the world's first wide-bodied commercial airliner and revolutionised air travel after coming into service in 1970. In interviews filmed on the original prototype, chief engineer Joe Sutter and his team describe the challenges they faced, from exploding engines to vibrations that threatened to tear the aircraft apart, and test pilot Brien Wygle reveals how on the first flight they hooked up car batteries as back-ups in case of engine failure

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher

programme.title: Africa Turns the Page: The Novels That Shaped a Continent
programme.desc: Nigerian-born presenter and historian David Olusoga explores the story of the African novel, and a time when writers including Chinua Achebe, Ngugi wa Thiong'o and Wole Soyinka became the conscience of a continent. In their wake, the African novel was to spread around the world - writers of the African diaspora such as Buchi Emecheta and Ben Okri created masterpieces from their adopted home of the United Kingdom. Featuring contributions by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, Aminatta Forna and 2019 Booker winner Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon examines how Denmark emerged from modest beginnings to become one of the greatest powers and arbiters of taste in northern Europe. He tells a story of incredible transformation befitting the homeland of Hans Christian Andersen, creator of The Ugly Duckling and The Emperor's New Clothes among many other fairy tales

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: Profiles of influential figures in the world of wood craftsmanship, focusing on famous furniture designer Thomas Chippendale. The film traces his journey from humble roots working as a joiner in Otley, West Yorkshire, to his success as a cabinet-maker in London

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is watering the plants on a hot day, and when the squirrels help everything gets wet and slippy

programme.title: Hey Duggee

They need to head to a nearby island but they are too poorly to sail the ship

programme.title: Twirlywoos

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Maddie, the Zoo and You

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug have a power cut in the bookshop. They want to know what it was like before electricity, so they travel to an old lighthouse. But before the keeper can return, the light threatens to go out! Our heroes manage to save the day with their head torches.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: A trip to see fireflies becomes a rescue when Xuli and Glitch get stuck in a swamp.

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama created by Roland Moore in 2009 to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the outbreak of the Second World War. As Britain struggles to contain the Nazi war machine, four females from different walks of life join the Women's Land Army, little realising their decision could change their futures. In the first episode, Nancy, who was forced to enlist, tries to get closer to Lord Hoxley at a dance. Starring Christine Bottomley, Summer Strallen, Becci Gemmell and Jo Woodcock, with Nathaniel Parker and Mark Benton

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine the history behind some of the nation's most famous dishes, beginning by exploring how the monarchy has influenced British cuisine. The duo also prepare a classic coronation chicken, and a beef dish named after Prince Albert, and food historian Ivan Day whips up a meal using a cookbook from the time of James II

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Detective Moses Mountree returns to Midsomer, an African warlord faces a new threat, and crooner Thaddeus Price sings a romantic song to his girlfriend

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The three remaining queens write, record and perform an all-new remix of a RuPaul classic as they battle for the title of Canada's first Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Independent Group MP Heidi Allen and stand-up Phil Wang joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell looks round a three-bedroom house in Worcester and Dion Dublin sees a tiny basement flat in Liverpool. Plus, a revisit to a London property first featured in 2016

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police officers in the Northeast target suspected drug gangs, and hold an auction of seized items. Plus, how property seized from criminals are used to fund children's activities

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Cornwall Showground in Wadebridge as Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items to sell at auction in Plymouth

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Annie learns Bea is pregnant, and enlists the aid of the farmer's son Billy to track down the father - a GI who refuses to take responsibility for his actions. Meanwhile, Tucker suspects Nancy of being an enemy sympathiser, and a dilemma concerning an injured horse drives a wedge between Lord Hoxley and his wife Ellen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the nation's love of bread, tracing its development over the course of centuries. In addition to trying their hands at bagel-making, they create their own version of summer pudding, and prepare a soup-and-roll combination

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps an aspiring writer from London to find a new home in Dumfries and Galloway, and also heads for the hills on a Scottish gold rush

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help the Rev Karen and husband Colin, who have £6,000 to turn their oddly-shaped and weed-filled garden in Romsey into an Italian retreat

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a bus conductor's ticket machine, a Victorian automaton that can play the piano, and a former world champion's kickboxing trophy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace and greengrocer Chris Bavin return to help more families with their food shopping, beginning with the Macbeth family in Windsor. Single mum Holly has her hands full looking after her two sons - 16-year-old Spencer and Fletcher, who is 11. Caring for Spencer, who has cerebral palsy, leaves Holly little time to cook, and as a result the family has become dependent on microwavable ready meals. While Spencer is happy to eat most foods, Fletcher - who has recently been diagnosed with autism - is fussier

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: Documentary following roller derby team the Tiger Bay Brawlers as they pursue a place in the European Championship, having come away with bronze medals in 2018. The season gets off to a bad start when star player Kim breaks her ankle, and they only have eight weeks to turn their fortunes around. A new recruit improves the team's attacking game, but leaves other players jostling for a place in the starting line-up

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Cases include Tia, who suffers from eczema and has tried all sorts of homeopathic remedies with little success, and Casey, who just wants his psoriasis under control

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Psoriasis sufferer Megan feels like she has exhausted all treatment options, while Rowanne is interested in learning about how to camouflage her port wine stain

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: Bolton's funniest stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports live from a specialist police rehab unit in Berkshire, and Rav Wilding finds out about the lengths farmers will go to protect animals

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A Merseyside drug dealer has his BMW seized and sold off at auction. Meanwhile, police in Derby are after a man they suspect is part of a 'county lines' drug operation

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Liverpool City Council's housing team investigate a rental property that is so dangerous for its residents it has to be immediately shut down

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Roo Irvine help two teams look for items in an antiques centre in the Lancashire town of Eccleston, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: After three months, Annie is still struggling to come to terms with Harry's death, Nancy and Lawrence embark on a full-blown affair and Joyce faces a dilemma about her husband. Esther fears Nancy may be a Nazi collaborator and food rations officer Ernest Luckhurst arrives at Finch's farm intent on catching him out

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history of the Vegetarian Society and discover how the movement changed public attitudes and became part of the mainstream. They also consider how British gardening has influenced the nation's cuisine, and provide a few recipes along the way

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple who have £500,000 to make a much-yearned-for leap to a country life in the Welsh borders. Along the way, the presenter visits 17th-century Tredegar House, now home to local artists' studios, and lends a hand making traditional Welsh musical instruments

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden with a dated patio into a modern, tranquil outdoor space for Amy and Kiran in Slough

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and a couple of gold plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Life at Colchester Zoo for curator Sarah Forsyth, who heads into the busiest summer of her life keeping her animals happy while four out of five of her keeping staff are furloughed. The documentary follows Sarah for two weeks, entirely inside the walls of one of Britain's largest zoos, as she works tirelessly to deal with the summer baby boom, animal emergencies and unique situations created by the absence of paying public

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels off-base for the high society wedding of Abigail's cousin. Raelle throws herself into her relationship with Scylla, who, unbeknownst to Raelle, faces an impossible choice. Abigail becomes disillusioned with her privilege while amongst the notable Bellweather line. Tally gets hit with two life-altering revelations that change the very fabric of the unit

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail has to come to terms with her vulnerability in the aftermath of a horrific event. Tally struggles with being supportive, while also keeping a secret from her unit. Raelle takes extreme measures to connect with Scylla

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express
