programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A luxury hotel chain makes penniless and exhausted couple Mike and Alison an offer they can't refuse, but the prospect of their departure drives a wedge between the ghosts, and the hardline Captain finds himself ostracised, while Pat is voted in as reluctant leader. programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Life at Colchester Zoo for curator Sarah Forsyth, who heads into the busiest summer of her life keeping her animals happy while four out of five of her keeping staff are furloughed. The documentary follows Sarah for two weeks, entirely inside the walls of one of Britain's largest zoos, as she works tirelessly to deal with the summer baby boom, animal emergencies and unique situations created by the absence of paying public

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels off-base for the high society wedding of Abigail's cousin. Raelle throws herself into her relationship with Scylla, who, unbeknownst to Raelle, faces an impossible choice. programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama created by Roland Moore in 2009 to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the outbreak of the Second World War. As Britain struggles to contain the Nazi war machine, four females from different walks of life join the Women's Land Army, little realising their decision could change their futures. In the first episode, Nancy, who was forced to enlist, tries to get closer to Lord Hoxley at a dance. Starring Christine Bottomley, Summer Strallen, Becci Gemmell and Jo Woodcock, with Nathaniel Parker and Mark Benton

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine the history behind some of the nation's most famous dishes, beginning by exploring how the monarchy has influenced British cuisine. The duo also prepare a classic coronation chicken, and a beef dish named after Prince Albert, and food historian Ivan Day whips up a meal using a cookbook from the time of James II

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. programme.desc: Northern Ireland v Norway. Stephen Watson presents action from the Group B1 encounter at Windsor Park. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: Obair Ealain (Street Art)
programme.desc: World famous artists arrive in Scotland from all corners of the globe for one of Europe's biggest street art events - Nuart Aberdeen. Edgy, stunning, provocative, subversive; their work makes an impact. These 12 international street artists have come to paint the grey streets of Aberdeen and they have only five days to complete their work before the festival opens

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: The Inverness-based Air Ambulance helicopter is called out to an injured hillwalker in the remote west coast and airlifts him to hospital. Plus, the Station Commander at RAF Lossiemouth flies a Typhoon and the Barra fire team launches their rescue boat

programme.title: Air Tòir Manachainn Dhèir (The Lost Monastery of Deer)
programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. This is the story of a team of archaeologists and volunteers who spent nine days hunting for the lost monastery of Deer, where the book was written. In the event, those involved found more than they imagined possible

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Sarah Jane learns the truth about Alasdair after she's delivered her news to a devastated Cailean

programme.title: Jimmy Johnstone
programme.desc: The life of the Scottish sporting star, telling the story of the highs and the lows of the working class footballer who died of motor neurone disease in 2006

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges including quiz rounds and trolley dashes to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Brian, Ravi, Vanessa and Shanice pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle is upset when Paddy confronts her for starting a family feud and is also annoyed to hear Paddy and Marlon talking about her and Jamie behind their backs. As Harriet heads off to a religious retreat, Dawn tries to calm a paranoid Will, who refuses to listen and places an urgent furtive phone call

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff shows Sally his true colours as he tries to assure Tim that he is the innocent party, while Dev shows an estate agent round the kebab shop, much to Asha and Aadi's concern. Shona spends the day with David in the salon and Daniel confides in Adam how he's worried about Nicky

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: Life in two of the UK's busiest animal hospitals, following the fortunes of the staff, volunteers and patients at the A&E departments of Blue Cross in London Victoria and Grimsby. Ambulance drivers Sonia and Angela pick up a 50kg Dogue de Bordeaux that has a mysterious bedwetting problem, which vet Cristina must get to the bottom of, while Lawrence tries to find out why a guinea pig is anxious and upset

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a celebration of stars who have made appearances on the soap over the years, including actor Ian McKellen, actress Joanna Lumley and comedian Peter Kay. There is also a look at actors who have crossed the soap divide, appearing in both Emmerdale and Coronation Street

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson returns with the big-prize quiz as six contestants compete for the chance to answer 15 questions that could change their lives and land them £1,000,000. The programme was filmed without a studio audience due to strict government guidelines around social distancing, but each competitor still has four lifelines, with 50:50, Ask the Host and two opportunities to Phone a Friend

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Terminal 4 turns blue, airside officer Hugh has a potential environmental mess on his hands, and an octogenarian forces herself into the country under the watchful eye of Passenger Experience Guru Demi. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: This episode focuses on products that have not changed in centuries, including Heck sausages, which are produced on a family farm in Bedale, North Yorkshire. There is also a look at the Maldon salt company, who have been producing sea salt since the late 19th century, and Glenfarclas single malt whisky, which has been made by the Grant family in the Scottish Highlands since 1865

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson returns with the big-prize quiz as six contestants compete for the chance to answer 15 questions that could change their lives and land them £1,000,000. The programme was filmed without a studio audience due to strict government guidelines around social distancing, but each competitor still has four lifelines, with 50:50, Ask the Host and two opportunities to Phone a Friend

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents a look back at the opening nine stages as the race reached its first rest day. Plus, reports from the riders' press conferences and a look ahead to week two. With analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen makes a one-pot chicken pilau rice with mango chutney and her own take on Eton mess using mangos and cardamom. Her Indian tour continues with a look at Rajasthani cuisine and a visit to the Fairmount Hotel in Jaipur. Plus, award-winning chef Vivek Singh reveals the inspirations behind his modern take on Indian food

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle is upset when Paddy confronts her for starting a family feud and is also annoyed to hear Paddy and Marlon talking about her and Jamie behind their backs. As Harriet heads off to a religious retreat, Dawn tries to calm a paranoid Will, who refuses to listen and places an urgent furtive phone call

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff shows Sally his true colours as he tries to assure Tim that he is the innocent party, while Dev shows an estate agent round the kebab shop, much to Asha and Aadi's concern. Shona spends the day with David in the salon and Daniel confides in Adam how he's worried about Nicky

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: Life in two of the UK's busiest animal hospitals, following the fortunes of the staff, volunteers and patients at the A&E departments of Blue Cross in London Victoria and Grimsby. Ambulance drivers Sonia and Angela pick up a 50kg Dogue de Bordeaux that has a mysterious bedwetting problem, which vet Cristina must get to the bottom of, while Lawrence tries to find out why a guinea pig is anxious and upset

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Jason Manford narrates a celebration of stars who have made appearances on the soap over the years, including actor Ian McKellen, actress Joanna Lumley and comedian Peter Kay. There is also a look at actors who have crossed the soap divide, appearing in both Emmerdale and Coronation Street

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson returns with the big-prize quiz as six contestants compete for the chance to answer 15 questions that could change their lives and land them £1,000,000. The programme was filmed without a studio audience due to strict government guidelines around social distancing, but each competitor still has four lifelines, with 50:50, Ask the Host and two opportunities to Phone a Friend

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Terminal 4 turns blue, airside officer Hugh has a potential environmental mess on his hands, and an octogenarian forces herself into the country under the watchful eye of Passenger Experience Guru Demi. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: This episode focuses on products that have not changed in centuries, including Heck sausages, which are produced on a family farm in Bedale, North Yorkshire. There is also a look at the Maldon salt company, who have been producing sea salt since the late 19th century, and Glenfarclas single malt whisky, which has been made by the Grant family in the Scottish Highlands since 1865

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights

With analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: Parveen makes a one-pot chicken pilau rice with mango chutney and her own take on Eton mess using mangos and cardamom. Her Indian tour continues with a look at Rajasthani cuisine and a visit to the Fairmount Hotel in Jaipur. Plus, award-winning chef Vivek Singh reveals the inspirations behind his modern take on Indian food

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Eugene and Dan Levy sit in as hosts, talking to their Schitt's Creek co-stars Catherine O'Hara and Annie Murphy

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A worn-down Amy reveals to Marlon that Al's threatening to sack her because of a missing delivery of pizza boxes, while Jamie overhears Sam bad-mouthing him. The social worker enters in the midst of a row between Charity and Sarah, and Belle arrives back at the Dingle's to collect her things after confessing to Zak that she's moving in with Jamie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff plays the victim once again, but Tim wonders if his dad is as innocent as he makes out, while Mary and Cathy discuss Dev's money problems. Daniel admits to Adam that he's been paying Nicky to dress up as Sinead so he could pretend she's still alive, and David assures Shona that she's improving all the time

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of viewers' videos, featuring a cat terrorising a postman and an accident at the top of the Eiffel Tower, as well as elaborate pranks. Plus, he also reveals what Judge Rinder does when he is not in court

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: The management introduce a new objective in which staff are asked to rush all purchases straight to customers' cars. Garrett is impressed by a visitor's hi-tech purchase

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Jonah and Sandra represent the staff at tricky union contract negotiations with the top bosses. Amy, Dina and Cheyenne distract themselves by looking for the perfect Christmas tree, while Mateo, Garrett and Glenn compete to see who can sell an unsellable item. Comedy, starring America Ferrera

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: Max and Michael begin to worry about Isobel after a series of mysterious blackouts cause her to spiral. Liz turns to Grant Green for help when she learns that he may have witnessed something strange on the night Rosa died, while Kyle and Alex uncover a secret that Kyle's father has been keeping from everyone

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Immie from Bath is given five menus constructed by a quintet of potential sweethearts, and must decide which three she would like to meet based on the meals they planned

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A worn-down Amy reveals to Marlon that Al's threatening to sack her because of a missing delivery of pizza boxes, while Jamie overhears Sam bad-mouthing him. programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a vlogger squirting perfume in her eyes and a runaway car wrecking a campsite

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Ellen is joined by talk-show host Steve Harvey, while Zoe Kravitz chats about her new TV comedy drama High Fidelity. Ray Romano plays Ellen's Burning Questions

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen's studio audience play her outrageously fun games including Don't Leave Me Hanging, Aw Snap!, Oh Ship! and One-Eyed Monster.

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Banana, Mouse, Frog, Taco and Kitty perform for guest judge Gabriel Iglesias and the rest of the panel. Nick Cannon presents as one of the singers is unmasked and sent home.

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: n London, model Elesha is hoping to be impressed by the style choices of three potential suitors. To impress her, they have to dress her, as Elesha has no other information about them but the outfits they buy. At the end of the challenge, which of the three competitors will she choose to take out on a date?

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness continues to arrange more dates for his 30 single women. This time, the 'love lift' delivers hypnotist Ben from London, Essex-based broker Harry, Edinburgh landscape gardener David and Paul, a 'body double' from Dublin. All four men are given a chance to win over Paddy's singletons, but to secure a date they must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Plus, an update on how last weeks' couples got on during their stay on the Isle of Fernandos

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a quad bike that ruins a wedding, an agoraphobic dog and a sensitive cat that hates being called fat

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey threatens to end things with Alan when he refuses to give her a key to the house, while Walden, Herb and Billy commiserate about their failed relationships. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Courtney Thorne

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake returns from the base with his girlfriend Tammy, and asks Walden for money so he can propose to her. However, the teenager has misunderstood his relationship with the tattoo shop owner, who has several children and just wants a bit of fun with no strings attached. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: US version of the hit reality show featuring a gang of gorgeous young singles gathered in one villa for the time of their lives. Summer's almost over but things are just starting to heat up, and the brand-new batch of Islanders move into a stunning villa, each one looking for a real romantic connection

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois gets a job as a postal worker to pay for Stewie's private school and finds an unsent letter from Peter that was meant for an old flame of his

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris interrupt Stewie's TV time, prompting the angry baby to go back in time to break up Peter and Lois so he's never conceived. However, he is instead reborn into a Downton Abbey-esque British household

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve tries to prove his toughness to Stan by adopting the persona of a superhero, while Francine also goes through an identity crisis when she receives news that could change the family for ever

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan finally saves enough money to join the golf club he has worked at for the past 30 summers - only to discover Steve has been given a membership before him. However, everything changes when the CIA officer learns who owns the business. Featuring the voice of Hulk Hogan

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland visits his old high school and learns there is no longer a baseball team, prompting him to raise money to rebuild the stadium before the season begins. However, he decides to take a more hands-on role in coaching the team when he discovers his son Cleveland Jr is more keen on solving maths riddles than pitching balls

programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. programme.desc: Frank finds someone has slashed his tyres and written the word 'murderer' on his car. Alan makes Terry an offer he can't refuse. The Cairns struggle with their financial problems

programme.desc: The Cairns worry about paying for building work on Woodside Farm. Biff tells Kathy that Linda is pregnant. Terry tells Alan that he has unbarred the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des struggles to get used to having a full house. Liz is horrified to find out the cost of her divorce bill. Sally is annoyed when Ida admits to clocking Tricia in at the factory

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia tries again to contact Terry. Stephen tells the Platts he'd like to take them to Canada. Becky is upset when she hears that Liz is Des's old flame

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the fatal shooting of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he walked home from football practice in Croxteth, Liverpool, caught in the crossfire of a feud between rival gangs. Detective Superintendent Dave Kelly is charged with bringing his teenage killer to justice, but though the assailant's identity is well known, finding the evidence to charge him proves to be a far more difficult challenge. Stephen Graham stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean encounters an old flame, prompting a jealous Sid to remember why he married her in the first place. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar finds himself in an awkward position when the police investigate a warehouse robbery. A lost property clear-out at the police station gives David a new hobby, but he soon manages to cause trouble. Starring Derek Fowlds and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Family and friends attend the funeral of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he is laid to rest, while a police raid at the home of Kevin Moody leads to the recovery of the gun used in the schoolboy's murder. Moody must choose between being labelled a grass and risking violent retribution or protecting the killer and facing jail time. But an unexpected result from the forensic tests on the firearm leaves the entire investigation hanging in the balance. Fact-based drama, starring Stephen Graham and Michael Moran

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles feud from the previous night continues. Frank shows Chris his plans for the health farm. Biff and Linda are the only mourners at Ron's funeral

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The residents of Aidensfield are terrorised by a blackmailer and Sergeant Merton is less than impressed by the investigative methods of the handsome newcomer PC Rob Walker. Meanwhile, Vernon embarks on a venture in the prestigious world of publishing, with the help of David and Bernie. Jonathan Kerrigan stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is delighted when Mike announces his intention to marry into wealth - until a close link between the two families is uncovered

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid forgets an important party - unfortunately the occasion is his silver wedding anniversary. Sid James stars

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank finds someone has slashed his tyres and written the word 'murderer' on his car. Alan makes Terry an offer he can't refuse. The Cairns struggle with their financial problems

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Cairns worry about paying for building work on Woodside Farm. Biff tells Kathy that Linda is pregnant. Terry tells Alan that he has unbarred the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des struggles to get used to having a full house. Liz is horrified to find out the cost of her divorce bill. Sally is annoyed when Ida admits to clocking Tricia in at the factory

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia tries again to contact Terry. Stephen tells the Platts he'd like to take them to Canada. Becky is upset when she hears that Liz is Des's old flame

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the fatal shooting of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he walked home from football practice in Croxteth, Liverpool, caught in the crossfire of a feud between rival gangs. Detective Superintendent Dave Kelly is charged with bringing his teenage killer to justice, but though the assailant's identity is well known, finding the evidence to charge him proves to be a far more difficult challenge. Stephen Graham stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean encounters an old flame, prompting a jealous Sid to remember why he married her in the first place. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: Sid's tries to join the pop scene make him a laughing stock and he realises the generation gap is wider than ever

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid permits Mike's dancer girlfriend to stay for a couple of days, but soon changes his mind when he finds out what sort of performer she really is. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George is told to mind his table manners when Mildred invites her sister round for a meal. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar finds himself in an awkward position when the police investigate a warehouse robbery. A lost property clear-out at the police station gives David a new hobby, but he soon manages to cause trouble. Starring Derek Fowlds and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda is worried when Biff is missing all night. Frank is shocked when he receives a phone call - and it's Kim on the other end. Mandy and Paddy share an intimate moment

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is worried when Nick won't talk about his life in London. Claire tells Des he'll have to give away some things from his single life

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin suggests to Gail that they think again about Canada. Tricia is furious when Sally won't let her do overtime. Carol brings Becky a framed photo of Jeff

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar finds himself in an awkward position when the police investigate a warehouse robbery. A lost property clear-out at the police station gives David a new hobby, but he soon manages to cause trouble. Starring Derek Fowlds and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Family and friends attend the funeral of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he is laid to rest, while a police raid at the home of Kevin Moody leads to the recovery of the gun used in the schoolboy's murder. Moody must choose between being labelled a grass and risking violent retribution or protecting the killer and facing jail time. But an unexpected result from the forensic tests on the firearm leaves the entire investigation hanging in the balance. Fact-based drama, starring Stephen Graham and Michael Moran

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day one of the St Leger Meeting from Doncaster, featuring five races including the 2.10 Scarborough Stakes and 3.15 Sceptre Stakes. Plus, two races from Kempton Park. With analysis from Kevin Blake and Jason Weaver, reports by Matt Chapman, Luke Harvey and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe is concerned when Frank doesn't pick her up from the airport. Kathy goes to see Nick in prison. Mandy is hurt when Paddy makes jokes about her family

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe feels guilty for not being there when her father needed her. Mandy is accused of shoplifting. Biff wonders what will happen to Frank's estate following his death

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is furious to receive a demand from Liz's solicitor. Deirdre is thrilled when Stephen asks her out for dinner. Stephen sees the Platts off as they fly to Canada

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally finds it hard adjusting to being a boss. Deirdre worries that Stephen only took her out out of pity. Kevin tells Tricia that Sally stood up for and stopped Mike sacking her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates a suspicious death with the help of CID and Walker begins to suspect a poacher may be involved. Vernon tempts fate by employing a new mechanic for the garage without asking Bernie and attempts to recreate a traditional dance at the town fete. Guest starring Hannah Gordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: When potential witness Claire Olssen is intimidated into changing her statement, the investigation team look for alternative ways to reinforce their case. As Steve returns to work, Kelly is offered a deal that tests his resolve

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.desc: Short sports film about this compelling sporting rivalry.

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The team are sent to Spain with orders to persuade a woman to return home to her father. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A crook arranges the conversion of replica firearms into the real thing and sells them on to small-time hoodlums. When one of the arms misfires killing both a policeman and its holder, Regan is intent on discovering the source before more innocent lives are lost. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mrs Peel is kidnapped by a mad Teutonic film director, who is attempting to make a film about her life. Unfortunately for Peel it ends with her death, leaving Steed in a hurry to save her. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying an East German car, a retired greyhound and a piano. He fears the items could destroy his reputation, leaving him with the job of selling them on without the rest of the manor finding out. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. To make matters worse, he discovers he is being stalked by an old friend who has embarked on a vendetta. Vicki Michelle ('Allo 'Allo!) makes an early screen appearance, alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage nine of the most prestigious race on the calendar, which took the riders on a mountainous 153km route from Pau to Laruns. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Paul is kidnapped and Harry and the Contessa are forced to assist in a theft in order to save him. Drama, guest starring Peter Bowles, with Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is invited to a party on an aeroplane with a motley crew. However, as the pilotless craft takes off, a voice tells them they are bound for a desert island where one will be revealed as a killer. Donald Sutherland, Charlotte Rampling and Brian Blessed guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents a look back at the opening nine stages as the race reached its first rest day. Plus, reports from the riders' press conferences and a look ahead to week two. With analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team travel to a venue in Hungary that has produced carp weighing more than 40kg in the past. However, they quickly discover it shares a common problem with many lakes in this part of the world - there are also hordes of hungry smaller carp under 20lb

programme.title: Platoon (1986)
programme.desc: A raw recruit is thrown into the thick of the Vietnam War along the Cambodian border, where he finds himself caught up in a lethal feud between two sergeants. Oliver Stone's Oscar-winning drama, the first of his Vietnam trilogy, starring Charlie Sheen, Willem Dafoe, Tom Berenger, Forest Whitaker, Kevin Dillon, Johnny Depp and John C McGinley

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mrs Peel is kidnapped by a mad Teutonic film director, who is attempting to make a film about her life. Unfortunately for Peel it ends with her death, leaving Steed in a hurry to save her. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying an East German car, a retired greyhound and a piano. He fears the items could destroy his reputation, leaving him with the job of selling them on without the rest of the manor finding out. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. programme.title: Tour de France Highlights

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Paul is kidnapped and Harry and the Contessa are forced to assist in a theft in order to save him. Drama, guest starring Peter Bowles, with Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is invited to a party on an aeroplane with a motley crew. However, as the pilotless craft takes off, a voice tells them they are bound for a desert island where one will be revealed as a killer. Donald Sutherland, Charlotte Rampling and Brian Blessed guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents a look back at the opening nine stages as the race reached its first rest day. Plus, reports from the riders' press conferences and a look ahead to week two. With analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team travel to a venue in Hungary that has produced carp weighing more than 40kg in the past. However, they quickly discover it shares a common problem with many lakes in this part of the world - there are also hordes of hungry smaller carp under 20lb

programme.title: Platoon (1986)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives roast chicken a makeover with a kimchi glaze and sauce, dressing it in toasted sesame seeds and serving with fluffy white rice and a refreshing slaw, to create a truly memorable meal. He then stuffs a salmon fillet with creamy prawns and wraps it in bacon, cooking it on a bed of greens for a traybake fit for family, friends and special occasions, and finishes off by rustling up a super-speedy, all-in-one cake with the flavours of pistachio, tahini and pomegranate

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: More from life behind the scenes at a busy public employment service. Penny used to run a multimillion pound retail chain in Nigeria with her husband, but since their divorce the 49-year-old has found herself living in Leeds on universal credit. Lindsey in the social justice team helps Dean prepare for a test which, if he passes, could lead to a job in the construction industry, while Afghan asylum seeker Zaref not only does needs a job, but has to earn more than £18,600 to bring his wife to the U

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the work of police in Northamptonshire, where, with resources stretched to breaking point, officers are struggling to meet the increased demand on their services. This edition looks at the impact of relationships on the force, with last year seeing were more than 13,000 domestic incidents in the county

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following life inside an A&E department. The series begins with the story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at the challenges the Devon and Cornwall police force face patrolling the 730 miles of coastline in their jurisdiction. Cornwall is famous for a long history of smuggling, but this criminal activity is not a thing of the past. In this episode, the police control room receives a call from a member of the public on Friday morning, claiming unusual cargo is being unloaded from a tiny boat into a waiting van. Soon 30 police units from across both counties have been deployed, but a shocking discovery is made when the vehicle is tracked down

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

A 17-strong team, with armed support, a dog unit and aerial assets, ventures into the surrounding woodland to discover where they have gone. programme.desc: Mike storms off over a disagreement with Molly, so she decides to go on a shopping spree at the mall. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane learsn that if she is not married within 24 hours, her mother with lose her inheritance from her late husband's estate. In light of such developments, Diane asks Sam to marry her on a strict business basis. Shelley Long stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Sam reacts to Diane's attraction to his brother by inviting her to go to Paris with him

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy invites her brother for dinner, but he ends up outstaying his welcome, forcing Robert to take desperate measures to eject him from the couch. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra begin telling lies in a bid to cover up previous falsehoods, but Marie soon suspects what they are doing. Comedy, with Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra realises Ray is planning something for her birthday and suggests having a Chinese banquet, but her mother has already started organising an old-fashioned English tea party. Katherine Helmond guest stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz asks Frasier to narrate her documentary on the history of space travel - but he makes such a hash of it she ends up getting rid of him and recruiting former astronaut John Glenn instead. Meanwhile, Niles tries to relive his childhood by entering a kite-flying competition

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier search for a wealthy patron prepared to give their old school a much-needed cash injection, and soon find the ideal candidate - Martin's new girlfriend. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce, with Barbara Babcock (Hill Street Blues)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Self-important 'corkmaster' Niles arranges a boycott of Frasier's new radio show on wine appreciation, while Martin struggles to escape the powerful clutches of his new physiotherapist - until he samples her delicious cooking. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jane Grote Abell, the Chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the company's founder, poses as a contestant on a reality TV show who is trying to win money to open her own restaurant. Her aim is to uncover where the company is going, what plans for the future should include and whether her father's values are still being upheld

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: River Cottage
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall returns to celebrate the selection of fare available over the autumn and winter seasons. After travelling to Gloucestershire for expert advice on making pear cider, he undergoes a crash course in professional free diving and takes to the open sea to find scallops. He also joins foraging guru John Wright to hunt for mushrooms in the woods, visits a mass tree planting day in the Peak District and uses the offal from his two-year-old steer to make a hearty stew

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with magician and comedian Paul Zenon joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to the seaside resort of Southport, where Harry comes across some old ammunition crates that he decides to turn into pieces of furniture

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Katrin and Thomas from London search for a holiday home in the countryside of inland Malaga, where they hope to one day live permanently and enjoy the Spanish lifestyle

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, three couples compete to host the best dinner party and win the £1,000 cash prize. Sharna and Silvar host first, with a menu of family favourites. On day two, Charlotte and Russ take their guests' taste buds to Tuscany with an array of classic Italian treats. Finally, Shannon and Max host a night of favourite flavours taking everyone 'around the world'

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Part two of two. Accusations fly thick and fast in the wake of Mr Burns's shooting, and it is up to Chief Wiggum to discover who pulled the trigger - but plenty of people in Springfield had a motive for wanting the old tyrant dead. Featuring the guest voice of Tito Puente

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, looking back at some of Chester's more wily residents that have proven themselves to be expert escapologists. When the Sumatran orangutans are moved to a new home, keepers discover on CCTV that four of the troupe have gone missing. Meanwhile, three-month-old Andean bear cub Madidi escapes into the paddock next door

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside an A&E department, focusing on patients and their families, as well as the work of the staff dealing with the emergencies. This episode follows the stories of three patients and how they cope when life takes an unexpected turn, including a patient who severed three of his fingers with an electric saw. Elsewhere, a man with motor neurone disease is rushed to St George's with severe sepsis, and a teenager struggles to come to terms with the impact of serious injuries he sustained in a car crash six weeks ago

programme.title: Can We Cure Kids' Cancer?
programme.desc: Following three children and their families as they undergo both cutting-edge and traditional treatments at the Royal Marsden in London. Cameras follow three-year-old Charlotte, who is one of the first children in the UK to take a pioneering new targeted drug that has saved her life, while toddler Artemis must undergo a stem cell transplant, first done over 50 years ago, as this is the best option available to her. Elsewhere Lily, who is 14, takes on a cocktail of chemotherapy and newer immunotherapy drugs

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Roxi and Glen are the first couple to play host in Southampton, where things get off to a bad start as all the others are dreading their cauliflower puree starter. On the second evening, Russell and Lisa's pork dish and salmon main get raised eyebrows from their guests, although the food is not the only problem as animosity is brewing between two of the women. Tennis fans John and Suzy bring the week to a close - can they serve up an ace menu and walk away with the prize?

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: With the majority of under-30s struggling to get on the property ladder and millions of over-60s sitting on empty rooms, young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together and seeing how they get along. In this edition, 21-year-old poet Marvell leaves his east London council house to lodge with retired couple Lynne and John in their converted chapel in the Somerset town of Glastonbury. Meanwhile, in Birmingham, 20-year-old Sophie co-habits with no-nonsense pensioner Eunice. How she will cope living under her landlord's strict house rules remains to be seen

programme.title: The Sessions (2012)
programme.desc: Writer Mark has survived polio, but still needs to use an iron lung. At the age of 38, he has begun a writing career but has never had sex. With the support of his parish priest, he enlists the help of a sex therapist in gaining the confidence to lose his virginity. Comedy drama, starring John Hawkes, Helen Hunt and William H Macy

programme.desc: The crimes of Steven Wright, also known as the Suffolk Strangler, who killed five female sex workers in Ipswich during 2006, and was sentenced to life imprisonment in February 2008. This programme tells the tale of his crimes, as well as the steps that have been put in place nationally to try to educate both sex workers and police, in an effort to minimise the risk of this type of crime occurring again

programme.title: Missing or Murdered?
programme.desc: A look at the case of 35-year-old chef Claudia Lawrence, who disappeared in 2009 sparking one of the most high profile missing persons cases of recent years. The programme examines how media coverage of Claudia's private life impacted on the police investigation, and analyses theories of what really happened, including a recent possible sighting of her in Amsterdam and her mother's fears of sex trafficking

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine the experiences of people who have become involved in disputes with neighbours. Ruth also talks to angel investor Lauren Maillian and Eamonn meets city stockbroker Andrew Craig to learn about the arcane workings of the world of high finance

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: The vets treat some of North Yorkshire's more exotic residents, including Amadeus the alpaca, whose owner Christina found him with a stick in his eye. Julian Norton knows he probably would not survive an operation to remove the eye, and the kindest thing might be to put him to sleep, but he is hoping to find another solution. programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried's younger brother Tristan joins the practice after graduating from Edinburgh veterinary college. James finds himself begrudgingly taking the new arrival with him on his rounds, which leads to a disastrous clash of over how to treat a cow suffering from milk fever. On their return to the practice, they find that Mrs Hall has guessed that Tristan is not being entirely honest about his sudden arrival. Elsewhere, James makes a good impression on a wealthy widow (Diana Rigg) and her beloved Pekingese

programme.title: Fergie & Andrew: The Duke & Duchess of Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary examining the scandals that have surrounded Prince Andrew and Sarah Ferguson since their marriage in 1986 and subsequent divorce. The programme examines the succession of PR gaffes and undercover tabloid stings that followed in the Duchess of York's wake once she was no longer part of the royal family, as well as looking into the prince's friendship with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein

programme.title: Carpenters: Their Greatest Hits
programme.desc: In a 14-year career, brother-and-sister duo Richard and Karen Carpenter won three Grammys and had 17 US Adult Contemporary Billboard chart number ones. Narrated by Ken Bruce, this programme features classic performances and official music videos of 16 songs featuring the much-loved duo. Each is accompanied by interesting facts about the act President Richard Nixon once described as 'Young America at its very best'

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: For years, London's East End was denied a new bridge because of the need to guarantee passage of ships up and down the Thames. For years, the greatest engineers of the age hunted for a design that would keep both road and water traffic moving. As Rob Bell explains, the solution came from architect Horace Jones, whose revolutionary design, with its thousand-tonne opening sections, was powered by nothing more than water. Rob also explains the bridge was lucky to survive the Second World War, having been a target for German V-1 flying bombs

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Close friends Janet Perrie and Fiona Hope moved to the South Ayrshire village of Dipple in search of an idyllic new life for themselves and their 22 dogs. Unfortunately for them, other residents were enraged by the noise, smell and mess and wasted no time in contacting the authorities. In a cul-de-sac in Leicestershire, the Pollard, Walne and Elliott families have been harassed by 68-year-old Leslie Collins for more than a decade after they complained about the noise from an illegal wood-waste business he was running from his home. He then began to target them with increasingly intimidating behaviour - including mooning, two-finger salutes, shouting abuse, cutting holes in hedges and spying on them with binoculars

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell tells the story behind the design and construction of Isambard Kingdom Brunel's famous bridge over the picturesque Avon gorge at Bristol, and discovers that some people question whether it was entirely the product of his own devising. The bridge remained unfinished in Brunel's lifetime, but when he died in 1859, his friends decided that the bridge should be completed as a memorial, using the chains from another of his suspension bridges that was being demolished

programme.title: Wildlife SOS

programme.title: Divine Designs

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: BSL Zone

Western, starring William Holden, Richard Widmark and Janice Rule

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (2014)
programme.desc: A reporter discovers four humanoid amphibians who live in the sewers of New York, and use their martial arts skills to fight crime. She helps them as they face their toughest challenge yet - defeating a sinister criminal organisation that seems to have power over the entire city. Fantasy adventure, starring Megan Fox and William Fichtner, with the voice of Johnny Knoxville

Action thriller sequel, with Bruce Willis, Timothy Olyphant, Justin Long and Maggie Q

Indian crime drama, starring Antony Varghese and Chemban Vinod Jose. In Malayalam

Horror thriller, starring Dominic Monaghan, Ksenia Solo and Jennette McCurdy
